<p><strong>Coursera and Udemy are now one company, creating one of the world's most comprehensive skills development platforms for the AI era. This strengthens our ability to acc...
interviewSteps: Portfolio Review Round (Detailed walk-through of past case studies)
Design whiteboard Challenge (Interactive problem solving in real-time)
Cross-functional Fit (Collaborating with PMs and Devs)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Practice describing your design process from research to implementation. Master typography, grid frameworks, hierarchy, design system governance, and Figma interactions. Understand digital accessibility guidelines (WCAG) and UX analytics (hotjar, user surveys).

prepResources.name: The Design of Everyday Things by Don Norman
prepResources.desc: Fundamental reading on human-centered product usability.

prepResources.name: Don't Make Me Think by Steve Krug
prepResources.desc: The bible of intuitive web usability and UX design.

prepResources.name: Laws of UX (lawsofux.com)
prepResources.desc: Collection of visual design laws and cognitive psychology principles.

mainlyAsked: Walk us through a design case study. What user research did you conduct, and how did user feedback change the final mockups?
How do you collaborate with engineering teams to ensure your responsive UI designs are implemented accurately?
How do you approach designing forms and navigation for AA or AAA web accessibility compliance (WCAG)?
Explain your system of component design in Figma. How do you construct reusable libraries and auto-layouts?

<div class="content-intro"><p><strong>About Coursera</strong></p>
<p><strong>Coursera and Udemy are now one company, creating one of the world's most comprehensive skills development platforms for the AI era. This strengthens our ability to acc...
interviewSteps: ML Engineering & Algorithms Test (Mathematics, NumPy, PyTorch/TensorFlow)
ML Systems Design Round (Data ingestion, training feedback, real-time inference)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master machine learning foundations (loss functions, optimization algorithms, regularization). Focus on deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, Self-Attention). Learn ML system architecture: serving models with low latency, model monitoring, vector search databases (Pinecone/Milvus), and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

prepResources.name: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
prepResources.desc: Covers classical and deep learning pipelines practically.

prepResources.name: Designing Machine Learning Systems by Chip Huyen
prepResources.desc: Best resource on deploying, updating, and monitoring ML systems.

prepResources.name: Fast.ai Practical Deep Learning for Coders
prepResources.desc: Top-down approach to coding state-of-the-art models immediately.

mainlyAsked: What is data leakage in machine learning, and how do you prevent it during split validations?
Explain self-attention mechanics in Transformer models. What are Key, Query, and Value matrices?
How would you design a real-time recommendation feed for millions of active users? Detail the architecture.
What is gradient descent? Explain vanishing and exploding gradients and how layers like Batch Normalization mitigate them.

interviewSteps: Business Case Study / Statistical Analysis Round (A/B testing, modeling)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Brush up on statistics (probability distributions, hypothesis testing, regression), SQL (aggregations, CTEs, subqueries), data visualization tools (Tableau, PowerBI, seaborn), and business metric formulations (churn rate, retention, LTV). Practice communicating numerical findings clearly.

prepResources.name: Practical Statistics for Data Scientists by Bruce & Bruce
prepResources.desc: Covers data analysis, A/B testing, and exploratory modeling clearly.

prepResources.name: Kaggle Competitions & Tutorials
prepResources.desc: Practical hands-on notebooks working with real analytical datasets.

prepResources.name: SQLZoo & LeetCode SQL Tracks
prepResources.desc: Great interactive resources for perfecting multi-table joins and data filtering.

mainlyAsked: How do you design and evaluate an A/B test? What factors determine sample size and statistical power?
Write a SQL query using window functions to find the month-over-month percentage change in active users.
How do you address missing values, outliers, and highly correlated variables in a raw dataset?
Explain the difference between precision, recall, and F1-score. When is recall more important than precision?

prepResources.name: Terraform Up & Running by Yevgeniy Brikman
prepResources.desc: Great guide to managing infrastructure as code reliably.

mainlyAsked: How does a Kubernetes scheduling loop work? What happens when a container fails its readiness probe?
Explain Canary deployment vs Blue/Green deployment, and how would you configure them using a load balancer?
How do you secure secrets management (e.g. HashiCorp Vault) inside automated build environments?
What is drift detection in Terraform, and how do you safely reconcile state files with actual cloud resources?

jobs.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or...
interviewSteps: Business Case Study / Statistical Analysis Round (A/B testing, modeling)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Brush up on statistics (probability distributions, hypothesis testing, regression), SQL (aggregations, CTEs, subqueries), data visualization tools (Tableau, PowerBI, seaborn), and business metric formulations (churn rate, retention, LTV). Practice communicating numerical findings clearly.

prepResources.name: Practical Statistics for Data Scientists by Bruce & Bruce
prepResources.desc: Covers data analysis, A/B testing, and exploratory modeling clearly.

prepResources.name: Kaggle Competitions & Tutorials
prepResources.desc: Practical hands-on notebooks working with real analytical datasets.

prepResources.name: SQLZoo & LeetCode SQL Tracks
prepResources.desc: Great interactive resources for perfecting multi-table joins and data filtering.

mainlyAsked: How do you design and evaluate an A/B test? What factors determine sample size and statistical power?
Write a SQL query using window functions to find the month-over-month percentage change in active users.
How do you address missing values, outliers, and highly correlated variables in a raw dataset?
Explain the difference between precision, recall, and F1-score. When is recall more important than precision?

prepResources.desc: Fundamental reading on human-centered product usability.

prepResources.name: Don't Make Me Think by Steve Krug

prepResources.name: Laws of UX (lawsofux.com)

mainlyAsked: Walk us through a design case study. What user research did you conduct, and how did user feedback change the final mockups?

Explain your system of component design in Figma. How do you construct reusable libraries and auto-layouts?

jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or...
interviewSteps: ML Engineering & Algorithms Test (Mathematics, NumPy, PyTorch/TensorFlow)
ML Systems Design Round (Data ingestion, training feedback, real-time inference)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master machine learning foundations (loss functions, optimization algorithms, regularization). Focus on deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, Self-Attention). Learn ML system architecture: serving models with low latency, model monitoring, vector search databases (Pinecone/Milvus), and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

prepResources.name: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
prepResources.desc: Covers classical and deep learning pipelines practically.

prepResources.name: Designing Machine Learning Systems by Chip Huyen
prepResources.desc: Best resource on deploying, updating, and monitoring ML systems.

prepResources.name: Fast.ai Practical Deep Learning for Coders
prepResources.desc: Top-down approach to coding state-of-the-art models immediately.

mainlyAsked: What is data leakage in machine learning, and how do you prevent it during split validations?
Explain self-attention mechanics in Transformer models. What are Key, Query, and Value matrices?
How would you design a real-time recommendation feed for millions of active users? Detail the architecture.
What is gradient descent? Explain vanishing and exploding gradients and how layers like Batch Normalization mitigate them.

prepResources.desc: Learn to identify and protect against common web vulnerabilities.

mainlyAsked: Explain the flow of JWT authentication. Where should tokens be stored securely?
What is CORS? How does a browser handle preflight requests, and how do you configure it in server middleware?

What is drift detection in Terraform, and how do you safely reconcile state files with actual cloud resources?

prepResources.name: The Design of Everyday Things by Don Norman
prepResources.desc: Fundamental reading on human-centered product usability.

prepResources.name: Don't Make Me Think by Steve Krug
prepResources.desc: The bible of intuitive web usability and UX design.

prepResources.name: Laws of UX (lawsofux.com)
prepResources.desc: Collection of visual design laws and cognitive psychology principles.

mainlyAsked: Walk us through a design case study. What user research did you conduct, and how did user feedback change the final mockups?
How do you collaborate with engineering teams to ensure your responsive UI designs are implemented accurately?
How do you approach designing forms and navigation for AA or AAA web accessibility compliance (WCAG)?
Explain your system of component design in Figma. How do you construct reusable libraries and auto-layouts?

What is drift detection in Terraform, and how do you safely reconcile state files with actual cloud resources?

jobs.role: Technical Escalations Engineer 2 (APM - Java/Proxy/C++) - Mexico City
jobs.description:
<p>As Datadog’s in-house product experts, the Technical Escalation Engineering (TEE) team plays a critical role in driving our global success. We enable our customers, from the world’s most innovative startups to the largest enterprises.</p>
<p>Through deep technical expertise, rel...
interviewSteps: Product Sense Case Study (Designing a new feature or strategy for a consumer base)
Technical & Analytical Round (API structures, query logic, metrics execution)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master product strategy frameworks (RICE for prioritization, SWOT analysis, User Personas). Understand analytical tools, product lifecycles, and agile release management. Focus on defining crisp metrics (North Star, retention, conversion funnels).

prepResources.name: Cracking the PM Interview by Gayle McDowell & Jackie Bavaro
prepResources.desc: Detailed guide covering PM roles, mock interviews, and sample questions.

prepResources.name: Decode and Conquer by Lewis C. Lin
prepResources.desc: Great visual framework tool for answering product design case studies.

prepResources.name: Product School Blog & Webinars
prepResources.desc: Excellent insights from active PMs at top Silicon Valley enterprises.

mainlyAsked: What is your favorite mobile application and how would you redesign it to increase active engagement?
How do you determine feature priority when your lead engineer wants stability but marketing wants a new release?
How would you measure the success of a newly launched user onboarding walkthrough feature?
Describe a product launch that failed. What did you learn, and what data did you use to pivot?

<p>私たちは、Datadogの社内プロダクト・エキスパートです。テクニカル・ソリューション・チームは、潜在的な顧客を教育し、既存の顧客を満足させ、成功させることで、Datadogの世界的な成長を可能にしています。私たちは、デモやプレゼンテーション、技術評価、継続的なサポートを通じて、技術や製品に関する専門知識をお客様と共有しています。テクニカル・ソリューションは、知識を共有し、技術的なスキルセットを継続的に向上させるために絶えず協力し合う、成長中のグローバル・チームです。</p>

interviewSteps: Mobile Coding Screen (Custom view rendering, list display with network fetch)
App Architecture & Engineering (Memory optimization, local storage, background processing)
Final Interview Loop (Behavioral and team collaboration)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Understand mobile application lifecycles, memory optimization, profiling tools (Xcode Instruments / Android Profiler), local databases (SQLite/CoreData/Room), and asynchronous networking. Be prepared to implement custom reactive UI elements in Swift/Kotlin or Flutter/React Native.

prepResources.name: Kodeco (formerly Ray Wenderlich) Mobile Guides
prepResources.desc: Top tutorials for iOS (Swift/SwiftUI) and Android (Kotlin/Jetpack Compose).

prepResources.name: Clean Architecture for Mobile Apps
prepResources.desc: Adapting architecture patterns like MVVM, VIPER, and MVI for mobile devices.

prepResources.name: Apple & Android Developer Documentation
prepResources.desc: Primary manuals covering UI lifecycles, background tasks, and OS APIs.

mainlyAsked: What is a retain cycle (memory leak) in mobile code, and how do weak/unowned references fix it?
How do you execute network requests off the main (UI) thread, and how do you marshal results back?
Explain mobile app state transitions. What happens when an app is sent to the background?
Design an offline-first mobile app dashboard that syncs with a server when connectivity returns.

jobs.role: Backend/API Engineer, Money as a Service
interviewSteps: Online Coding Challenge / DSA Screen (LeetCode medium complexity)
System Design & Scalability Round (Microservices, database selection, messaging queues)
Final Integration & Fit (Behavioral questions and technical leadership alignment)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master core backend system design: SQL vs NoSQL trade-offs, indexing strategies, caching patterns (Redis/Memcached), message brokers (Kafka/RabbitMQ), and API protocols (REST, GraphQL, gRPC). Understand concurrency patterns (goroutines, async/await event-loop, threads) of your preferred stack.

prepResources.name: Designing Data-Intensive Applications by Martin Kleppmann
prepResources.desc: The gold standard book on databases, storage, and distributed systems.

prepResources.name: System Design Primer by Donne Martin (GitHub)
prepResources.desc: Comprehensive syllabus covering scalable architectures and protocols.

prepResources.name: ByteByteGo by Alex Xu
prepResources.desc: Visual breakdowns of large-scale system design patterns and real-world case studies.

mainlyAsked: How do database indexes (B-Trees vs Hash indexes) improve read speed, and what is the write penalty?
Explain ACID transactions vs BASE consistency models in distributed databases.
How would you implement a distributed rate-limiting system across thousands of containers?
Design a system like TinyURL or Pastebin (API layout, database schema, and scaling plan).

jobs.prepRoadmap: Practice describing your design process from research to implementation. Master typography, grid frameworks, hierarchy, design system governance, and Figma interactions. Understand digital accessibility guidelines (WCAG) and UX analytics (hotjar, user surveys).

prepResources.name: The Design of Everyday Things by Don Norman
prepResources.desc: Fundamental reading on human-centered product usability.

prepResources.name: Don't Make Me Think by Steve Krug
prepResources.desc: The bible of intuitive web usability and UX design.

prepResources.name: Laws of UX (lawsofux.com)
prepResources.desc: Collection of visual design laws and cognitive psychology principles.

mainlyAsked: Walk us through a design case study. What user research did you conduct, and how did user feedback change the final mockups?
How do you collaborate with engineering teams to ensure your responsive UI designs are implemented accurately?
How do you approach designing forms and navigation for AA or AAA web accessibility compliance (WCAG)?
Explain your system of component design in Figma. How do you construct reusable libraries and auto-layouts?

interviewSteps: Technical Screening (Online coding challenge, basic DSA)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Solidify core Computer Science fundamentals: time/space complexities (Big O), data structures (arrays, hash maps, binary trees, graphs), sorting/searching patterns, object-oriented design principles, and basic web architecture concepts (DNS, HTTP cache, load balancers).

prepResources.name: Cracking the Coding Interview by Gayle McDowell
prepResources.desc: Standard text for preparing coding, algorithms, and behavioral questions.

prepResources.name: Grokking the System Design Interview
prepResources.desc: Visual introduction to scalability, caching, CDNs, and database layers.

prepResources.name: LeetCode Blind 75 / Grind 75 Patterns
prepResources.desc: Curated lists of algorithmic topics grouped by pattern taxonomy.

mainlyAsked: Write a function to detect cycle loops in a directed graph structure.
Explain key Object-Oriented design patterns (Singleton, Factory, Observer) and when to apply them.
What happens when you type a URL into a browser address bar and press Enter? Explain end-to-end.
Design a simple Pastebin or paste-sharing service. Detail the API schemas and write scaling calculations.

interviewSteps: Scripting & System Internals Test (Python/Go/Bash, Linux diagnostics)
Infrastructure Architecture Round (Kubernetes, AWS/GCP config, IaC)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Focus on modern infrastructure automation (Terraform, Ansible), container scheduling (Kubernetes concepts like Pods, Services, Deployments, ingress, and PVs), CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI), Linux server administration, networking models, and logging/monitoring systems (Prometheus, ELK).

prepResources.name: DevOps Roadmap (roadmap.sh)
prepResources.desc: Visual guide mapping out technologies required for DevOps engineers.

prepResources.name: Kubernetes Up & Running by Kelsey Hightower
prepResources.desc: Practical hands-on guide to container runtime orchestration.

prepResources.name: Terraform Up & Running by Yevgeniy Brikman
prepResources.desc: Great guide to managing infrastructure as code reliably.

mainlyAsked: How does a Kubernetes scheduling loop work? What happens when a container fails its readiness probe?
Explain Canary deployment vs Blue/Green deployment, and how would you configure them using a load balancer?
How do you secure secrets management (e.g. HashiCorp Vault) inside automated build environments?
What is drift detection in Terraform, and how do you safely reconcile state files with actual cloud resources?

interviewSteps: Business Case Study / Statistical Analysis Round (A/B testing, modeling)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Brush up on statistics (probability distributions, hypothesis testing, regression), SQL (aggregations, CTEs, subqueries), data visualization tools (Tableau, PowerBI, seaborn), and business metric formulations (churn rate, retention, LTV). Practice communicating numerical findings clearly.

prepResources.name: Practical Statistics for Data Scientists by Bruce & Bruce
prepResources.desc: Covers data analysis, A/B testing, and exploratory modeling clearly.

prepResources.name: Kaggle Competitions & Tutorials
prepResources.desc: Practical hands-on notebooks working with real analytical datasets.

prepResources.name: SQLZoo & LeetCode SQL Tracks
prepResources.desc: Great interactive resources for perfecting multi-table joins and data filtering.

mainlyAsked: How do you design and evaluate an A/B test? What factors determine sample size and statistical power?
Write a SQL query using window functions to find the month-over-month percentage change in active users.
How do you address missing values, outliers, and highly correlated variables in a raw dataset?
Explain the difference between precision, recall, and F1-score. When is recall more important than precision?

What is drift detection in Terraform, and how do you safely reconcile state files with actual cloud resources?

jobs.description:
<p><strong>About Stripe</strong></p>
<p>Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business...
interviewSteps: ML Engineering & Algorithms Test (Mathematics, NumPy, PyTorch/TensorFlow)
ML Systems Design Round (Data ingestion, training feedback, real-time inference)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master machine learning foundations (loss functions, optimization algorithms, regularization). Focus on deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, Self-Attention). Learn ML system architecture: serving models with low latency, model monitoring, vector search databases (Pinecone/Milvus), and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

prepResources.name: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
prepResources.desc: Covers classical and deep learning pipelines practically.

prepResources.name: Designing Machine Learning Systems by Chip Huyen
prepResources.desc: Best resource on deploying, updating, and monitoring ML systems.

prepResources.name: Fast.ai Practical Deep Learning for Coders
prepResources.desc: Top-down approach to coding state-of-the-art models immediately.

mainlyAsked: What is data leakage in machine learning, and how do you prevent it during split validations?
Explain self-attention mechanics in Transformer models. What are Key, Query, and Value matrices?
How would you design a real-time recommendation feed for millions of active users? Detail the architecture.
What is gradient descent? Explain vanishing and exploding gradients and how layers like Batch Normalization mitigate them.

App Architecture & Engineering (Memory optimization, local storage, background processing)
Final Interview Loop (Behavioral and team collaboration)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Understand mobile application lifecycles, memory optimization, profiling tools (Xcode Instruments / Android Profiler), local databases (SQLite/CoreData/Room), and asynchronous networking. Be prepared to implement custom reactive UI elements in Swift/Kotlin or Flutter/React Native.

prepResources.name: Kodeco (formerly Ray Wenderlich) Mobile Guides
prepResources.desc: Top tutorials for iOS (Swift/SwiftUI) and Android (Kotlin/Jetpack Compose).

prepResources.name: Clean Architecture for Mobile Apps
prepResources.desc: Adapting architecture patterns like MVVM, VIPER, and MVI for mobile devices.

prepResources.name: Apple & Android Developer Documentation
prepResources.desc: Primary manuals covering UI lifecycles, background tasks, and OS APIs.

mainlyAsked: What is a retain cycle (memory leak) in mobile code, and how do weak/unowned references fix it?
How do you execute network requests off the main (UI) thread, and how do you marshal results back?
Explain mobile app state transitions. What happens when an app is sent to the background?
Design an offline-first mobile app dashboard that syncs with a server when connectivity returns.

interviewSteps: Technical Screening (Online coding challenge, basic DSA)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Solidify core Computer Science fundamentals: time/space complexities (Big O), data structures (arrays, hash maps, binary trees, graphs), sorting/searching patterns, object-oriented design principles, and basic web architecture concepts (DNS, HTTP cache, load balancers).

prepResources.name: Cracking the Coding Interview by Gayle McDowell
prepResources.desc: Standard text for preparing coding, algorithms, and behavioral questions.

prepResources.name: Grokking the System Design Interview
prepResources.desc: Visual introduction to scalability, caching, CDNs, and database layers.

prepResources.name: LeetCode Blind 75 / Grind 75 Patterns
prepResources.desc: Curated lists of algorithmic topics grouped by pattern taxonomy.

mainlyAsked: Write a function to detect cycle loops in a directed graph structure.
Explain key Object-Oriented design patterns (Singleton, Factory, Observer) and when to apply them.
What happens when you type a URL into a browser address bar and press Enter? Explain end-to-end.
Design a simple Pastebin or paste-sharing service. Detail the API schemas and write scaling calculations.

interviewSteps: Business Case Study / Statistical Analysis Round (A/B testing, modeling)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Brush up on statistics (probability distributions, hypothesis testing, regression), SQL (aggregations, CTEs, subqueries), data visualization tools (Tableau, PowerBI, seaborn), and business metric formulations (churn rate, retention, LTV). Practice communicating numerical findings clearly.

prepResources.name: Practical Statistics for Data Scientists by Bruce & Bruce
prepResources.desc: Covers data analysis, A/B testing, and exploratory modeling clearly.

prepResources.name: Kaggle Competitions & Tutorials
prepResources.desc: Practical hands-on notebooks working with real analytical datasets.

prepResources.name: SQLZoo & LeetCode SQL Tracks
prepResources.desc: Great interactive resources for perfecting multi-table joins and data filtering.

mainlyAsked: How do you design and evaluate an A/B test? What factors determine sample size and statistical power?
Write a SQL query using window functions to find the month-over-month percentage change in active users.
How do you address missing values, outliers, and highly correlated variables in a raw dataset?
Explain the difference between precision, recall, and F1-score. When is recall more important than precision?

prepResources.name: Don't Make Me Think by Steve Krug

prepResources.desc: Collection of visual design laws and cognitive psychology principles.

interviewSteps: ML Engineering & Algorithms Test (Mathematics, NumPy, PyTorch/TensorFlow)
ML Systems Design Round (Data ingestion, training feedback, real-time inference)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master machine learning foundations (loss functions, optimization algorithms, regularization). Focus on deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, Self-Attention). Learn ML system architecture: serving models with low latency, model monitoring, vector search databases (Pinecone/Milvus), and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

prepResources.name: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
prepResources.desc: Covers classical and deep learning pipelines practically.

prepResources.name: Designing Machine Learning Systems by Chip Huyen
prepResources.desc: Best resource on deploying, updating, and monitoring ML systems.

prepResources.name: Fast.ai Practical Deep Learning for Coders
prepResources.desc: Top-down approach to coding state-of-the-art models immediately.

mainlyAsked: What is data leakage in machine learning, and how do you prevent it during split validations?
Explain self-attention mechanics in Transformer models. What are Key, Query, and Value matrices?

What is gradient descent? Explain vanishing and exploding gradients and how layers like Batch Normalization mitigate them.

jobs.description:
<h3><strong>About the Role</strong></h3>
<p>Together AI is building high-performance inference compute and the software platform around it. We’re looking for an early-career Analytics Engineer with strong fundamentals and high growth potential to grow into a technical l...
interviewSteps: Business Case Study / Statistical Analysis Round (A/B testing, modeling)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Brush up on statistics (probability distributions, hypothesis testing, regression), SQL (aggregations, CTEs, subqueries), data visualization tools (Tableau, PowerBI, seaborn), and business metric formulations (churn rate, retention, LTV). Practice communicating numerical findings clearly.

prepResources.name: Practical Statistics for Data Scientists by Bruce & Bruce
prepResources.desc: Covers data analysis, A/B testing, and exploratory modeling clearly.

prepResources.name: Kaggle Competitions & Tutorials
prepResources.desc: Practical hands-on notebooks working with real analytical datasets.

prepResources.name: SQLZoo & LeetCode SQL Tracks
prepResources.desc: Great interactive resources for perfecting multi-table joins and data filtering.

mainlyAsked: How do you design and evaluate an A/B test? What factors determine sample size and statistical power?
Write a SQL query using window functions to find the month-over-month percentage change in active users.
How do you address missing values, outliers, and highly correlated variables in a raw dataset?
Explain the difference between precision, recall, and F1-score. When is recall more important than precision?

jobs.description:
<h3><strong>About the role</strong></h3>
<p>Together AI/Codesandbox is looking for a Senior Backend/Distributed Systems engineer to help us build and maintain the codebase that powers the Together AI Sandbox service. This is a role for engineers that are familiar with s...
interviewSteps: Online Coding Challenge / DSA Screen (LeetCode medium complexity)
System Design & Scalability Round (Microservices, database selection, messaging queues)
Final Integration & Fit (Behavioral questions and technical leadership alignment)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master core backend system design: SQL vs NoSQL trade-offs, indexing strategies, caching patterns (Redis/Memcached), message brokers (Kafka/RabbitMQ), and API protocols (REST, GraphQL, gRPC). Understand concurrency patterns (goroutines, async/await event-loop, threads) of your preferred stack.

prepResources.name: Designing Data-Intensive Applications by Martin Kleppmann
prepResources.desc: The gold standard book on databases, storage, and distributed systems.

prepResources.name: System Design Primer by Donne Martin (GitHub)
prepResources.desc: Comprehensive syllabus covering scalable architectures and protocols.

prepResources.name: ByteByteGo by Alex Xu
prepResources.desc: Visual breakdowns of large-scale system design patterns and real-world case studies.

mainlyAsked: How do database indexes (B-Trees vs Hash indexes) improve read speed, and what is the write penalty?
Explain ACID transactions vs BASE consistency models in distributed databases.
How would you implement a distributed rate-limiting system across thousands of containers?
Design a system like TinyURL or Pastebin (API layout, database schema, and scaling plan).

jobs.prepRoadmap: Master machine learning foundations (loss functions, optimization algorithms, regularization). Focus on deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, Self-Attention). Learn ML system architecture: serving models with low latency, model monitoring, vector search databases (Pinecone/Milvus), and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

prepResources.desc: Covers classical and deep learning pipelines practically.

prepResources.name: Designing Machine Learning Systems by Chip Huyen
prepResources.desc: Best resource on deploying, updating, and monitoring ML systems.

prepResources.name: Fast.ai Practical Deep Learning for Coders
prepResources.desc: Top-down approach to coding state-of-the-art models immediately.

mainlyAsked: What is data leakage in machine learning, and how do you prevent it during split validations?
Explain self-attention mechanics in Transformer models. What are Key, Query, and Value matrices?
How would you design a real-time recommendation feed for millions of active users? Detail the architecture.
What is gradient descent? Explain vanishing and exploding gradients and how layers like Batch Normalization mitigate them.

jobs.description:
<h3><strong>About The Role</strong></h3>
<p>Together AI is building its infrastructure footprint at scale, and this role is central to making that happen. As an Infrastructure Design Engineer, you will own the design, planning, and technical execution of whitespace envi...
interviewSteps: Scripting & System Internals Test (Python/Go/Bash, Linux diagnostics)
Infrastructure Architecture Round (Kubernetes, AWS/GCP config, IaC)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Focus on modern infrastructure automation (Terraform, Ansible), container scheduling (Kubernetes concepts like Pods, Services, Deployments, ingress, and PVs), CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI), Linux server administration, networking models, and logging/monitoring systems (Prometheus, ELK).

prepResources.name: DevOps Roadmap (roadmap.sh)
prepResources.desc: Visual guide mapping out technologies required for DevOps engineers.

prepResources.name: Kubernetes Up & Running by Kelsey Hightower
prepResources.desc: Practical hands-on guide to container runtime orchestration.

prepResources.name: Terraform Up & Running by Yevgeniy Brikman
prepResources.desc: Great guide to managing infrastructure as code reliably.

mainlyAsked: How does a Kubernetes scheduling loop work? What happens when a container fails its readiness probe?
Explain Canary deployment vs Blue/Green deployment, and how would you configure them using a load balancer?
How do you secure secrets management (e.g. HashiCorp Vault) inside automated build environments?
What is drift detection in Terraform, and how do you safely reconcile state files with actual cloud resources?

interviewSteps: Scripting & System Internals Test (Python/Go/Bash, Linux diagnostics)
Infrastructure Architecture Round (Kubernetes, AWS/GCP config, IaC)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Focus on modern infrastructure automation (Terraform, Ansible), container scheduling (Kubernetes concepts like Pods, Services, Deployments, ingress, and PVs), CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI), Linux server administration, networking models, and logging/monitoring systems (Prometheus, ELK).

prepResources.name: DevOps Roadmap (roadmap.sh)
prepResources.desc: Visual guide mapping out technologies required for DevOps engineers.

prepResources.name: Kubernetes Up & Running by Kelsey Hightower
prepResources.desc: Practical hands-on guide to container runtime orchestration.

prepResources.name: Terraform Up & Running by Yevgeniy Brikman
prepResources.desc: Great guide to managing infrastructure as code reliably.

mainlyAsked: How does a Kubernetes scheduling loop work? What happens when a container fails its readiness probe?
Explain Canary deployment vs Blue/Green deployment, and how would you configure them using a load balancer?
How do you secure secrets management (e.g. HashiCorp Vault) inside automated build environments?
What is drift detection in Terraform, and how do you safely reconcile state files with actual cloud resources?

interviewSteps: ML Engineering & Algorithms Test (Mathematics, NumPy, PyTorch/TensorFlow)
ML Systems Design Round (Data ingestion, training feedback, real-time inference)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master machine learning foundations (loss functions, optimization algorithms, regularization). Focus on deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, Self-Attention). Learn ML system architecture: serving models with low latency, model monitoring, vector search databases (Pinecone/Milvus), and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

prepResources.name: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
prepResources.desc: Covers classical and deep learning pipelines practically.

prepResources.name: Designing Machine Learning Systems by Chip Huyen
prepResources.desc: Best resource on deploying, updating, and monitoring ML systems.

prepResources.name: Fast.ai Practical Deep Learning for Coders
prepResources.desc: Top-down approach to coding state-of-the-art models immediately.

mainlyAsked: What is data leakage in machine learning, and how do you prevent it during split validations?
Explain self-attention mechanics in Transformer models. What are Key, Query, and Value matrices?
How would you design a real-time recommendation feed for millions of active users? Detail the architecture.
What is gradient descent? Explain vanishing and exploding gradients and how layers like Batch Normalization mitigate them.

jobs.description:
<div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About HeyGen</strong></h2>
<p>At HeyGen, our mission is to make visual storytelling accessible to all. Over the last decade, visual content has become the preferred method of information creation, consumption, and r...
interviewSteps: Online Coding Challenge / DSA Screen (LeetCode medium complexity)
System Design & Scalability Round (Microservices, database selection, messaging queues)
Final Integration & Fit (Behavioral questions and technical leadership alignment)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master core backend system design: SQL vs NoSQL trade-offs, indexing strategies, caching patterns (Redis/Memcached), message brokers (Kafka/RabbitMQ), and API protocols (REST, GraphQL, gRPC). Understand concurrency patterns (goroutines, async/await event-loop, threads) of your preferred stack.

prepResources.name: Designing Data-Intensive Applications by Martin Kleppmann
prepResources.desc: The gold standard book on databases, storage, and distributed systems.

prepResources.name: System Design Primer by Donne Martin (GitHub)
prepResources.desc: Comprehensive syllabus covering scalable architectures and protocols.

prepResources.name: ByteByteGo by Alex Xu
prepResources.desc: Visual breakdowns of large-scale system design patterns and real-world case studies.

mainlyAsked: How do database indexes (B-Trees vs Hash indexes) improve read speed, and what is the write penalty?
Explain ACID transactions vs BASE consistency models in distributed databases.
How would you implement a distributed rate-limiting system across thousands of containers?
Design a system like TinyURL or Pastebin (API layout, database schema, and scaling plan).

interviewSteps: Business Case Study / Statistical Analysis Round (A/B testing, modeling)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Brush up on statistics (probability distributions, hypothesis testing, regression), SQL (aggregations, CTEs, subqueries), data visualization tools (Tableau, PowerBI, seaborn), and business metric formulations (churn rate, retention, LTV). Practice communicating numerical findings clearly.

prepResources.name: Practical Statistics for Data Scientists by Bruce & Bruce
prepResources.desc: Covers data analysis, A/B testing, and exploratory modeling clearly.

prepResources.name: Kaggle Competitions & Tutorials
prepResources.desc: Practical hands-on notebooks working with real analytical datasets.

prepResources.name: SQLZoo & LeetCode SQL Tracks
prepResources.desc: Great interactive resources for perfecting multi-table joins and data filtering.

mainlyAsked: How do you design and evaluate an A/B test? What factors determine sample size and statistical power?
Write a SQL query using window functions to find the month-over-month percentage change in active users.
How do you address missing values, outliers, and highly correlated variables in a raw dataset?
Explain the difference between precision, recall, and F1-score. When is recall more important than precision?

jobs.location: San Francisco, Palo Alto, Los Angeles, Toronto
jobs.description:
<div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About HeyGen</strong></h2>
<p>At HeyGen, our mission is to make visual storytelling accessible to all. Over the last decade, visual content has become the preferred method of information creation, consumption, and r...
interviewSteps: Initial Recruiter Screening (CV check & culture alignment)
Technical Screening (Basic JavaScript/TypeScript, CSS layouts, and live coding)
Frontend System Design Round (Component architecture, state management, and web performance)
Final Interview Loop (Behavioral, hiring manager, and coding challenges)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Deepen your knowledge of raw JavaScript/TypeScript internals (closures, event loop, promises, prototypes). Study modern web performance metrics (Core Web Vitals), state management strategies, and frontend routing architecture. Practice building mock responsive interfaces or utility widgets without external libraries.

prepResources.name: You Don't Know JS by Kyle Simpson
prepResources.desc: Best resource for mastering core JavaScript engine concepts.

prepResources.name: MDN Web Docs - Client-side Web APIs & Security
prepResources.desc: The definitive reference for browser APIs, DOM tree, and CORS.

prepResources.name: Frontend Masters - Advanced Web Performance & Architecture
prepResources.desc: High-level web architecture, build step optimization, and rendering pipelines.

mainlyAsked: How does the JavaScript Event Loop work? Describe task vs microtask queues.
Explain React reconciliation, fiber architecture, and key attributes in lists.
How would you optimize Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) and Interaction to Next Paint (INP)?
Design a custom autocomplete search dropdown widget with debouncing and keyboard navigation.

jobs.location: San Francisco, Palo Alto, Los Angeles, Toronto
jobs.description:

interviewSteps: Initial Interview (Resume walkthrough and stack preview)
Full-Stack Live Coding (Building a dynamic front-to-back widget)
System Architecture & API Integration (Auth flows, SQL modeling, state sync)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Ensure you are competent in both client-side rendering/state-sync and backend REST/GraphQL API construction. Learn web security basics (JWT, OAuth2, Session cookies, CORS, XSS, CSRF). Focus on designing end-to-end schemas for transactional database features.

prepResources.name: Full Stack Open (University of Helsinki)
prepResources.desc: Excellent deep dive into React, Node.js, Express, databases, and CI/CD.

prepResources.name: The Odin Project - Full Stack JavaScript Path
prepResources.desc: Hands-on projects covering full development lifecycles.

prepResources.desc: Learn to identify and protect against common web vulnerabilities.

mainlyAsked: Explain the flow of JWT authentication. Where should tokens be stored securely?
What is CORS? How does a browser handle preflight requests, and how do you configure it in server middleware?
Compare Server-Side Rendering (SSR) with Client-Side Rendering (CSR) regarding SEO, TTFB, and LCP.
Design a relational database schema for a multi-tenant project manager app with projects, tasks, and members.

jobs.prepRoadmap: Master machine learning foundations (loss functions, optimization algorithms, regularization). Focus on deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, Self-Attention). How would you design a real-time recommendation feed for millions of active users? Detail the architecture.
What is gradient descent? Explain vanishing and exploding gradients and how layers like Batch Normalization mitigate them.

jobs.description:
<div class="content-intro"><p><strong>About PhonePe Limited:</strong></p>
<p>Headquartered in India, its flagship product, the PhonePe digital payments app, was launched in Aug 2016. As of April 2025, PhonePe has over 60 Crore (600 Million) registered us...
interviewSteps: Mobile Coding Screen (Custom view rendering, list display with network fetch)
App Architecture & Engineering (Memory optimization, local storage, background processing)
Final Interview Loop (Behavioral and team collaboration)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Understand mobile application lifecycles, memory optimization, profiling tools (Xcode Instruments / Android Profiler), local databases (SQLite/CoreData/Room), and asynchronous networking. Be prepared to implement custom reactive UI elements in Swift/Kotlin or Flutter/React Native.

prepResources.name: Kodeco (formerly Ray Wenderlich) Mobile Guides
prepResources.desc: Top tutorials for iOS (Swift/SwiftUI) and Android (Kotlin/Jetpack Compose).

prepResources.name: Clean Architecture for Mobile Apps
prepResources.desc: Adapting architecture patterns like MVVM, VIPER, and MVI for mobile devices.

prepResources.name: Apple & Android Developer Documentation
prepResources.desc: Primary manuals covering UI lifecycles, background tasks, and OS APIs.

mainlyAsked: What is a retain cycle (memory leak) in mobile code, and how do weak/unowned references fix it?
How do you execute network requests off the main (UI) thread, and how do you marshal results back?
Explain mobile app state transitions. What happens when an app is sent to the background?
Design an offline-first mobile app dashboard that syncs with a server when connectivity returns.

interviewSteps: ML Engineering & Algorithms Test (Mathematics, NumPy, PyTorch/TensorFlow)
ML Systems Design Round (Data ingestion, training feedback, real-time inference)
jobs.prepRoadmap: Master machine learning foundations (loss functions, optimization algorithms, regularization). Focus on deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, Self-Attention). Learn ML system architecture: serving models with low latency, model monitoring, vector search databases (Pinecone/Milvus), and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

prepResources.name: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
prepResources.desc: Covers classical and deep learning pipelines practically.

prepResources.name: Designing Machine Learning Systems by Chip Huyen
prepResources.desc: Best resource on deploying, updating, and monitoring ML systems.

prepResources.name: Fast.ai Practical Deep Learning for Coders
prepResources.desc: Top-down approach to coding state-of-the-art models immediately.

mainlyAsked: What is data leakage in machine learning, and how do you prevent it during split validations?
Explain self-attention mechanics in Transformer models. What are Key, Query, and Value matrices?
How would you design a real-time recommendation feed for millions of active users? Detail the architecture.
What is gradient descent? Explain vanishing and exploding gradients and how layers like Batch Normalization mitigate them.
